description: Fault filter returns Continue instead of StopIteration when a delay timer is active. The filter chain continues processing while the delay timer is still pending. When postDelayInjection() fires and calls continueDecoding() via filter_manager.cc, the stream has already progressed past this filter, causing an assertion failure or undefined behavior in the filter manager's iteration state.

Header matching logic inverted in HeaderUtility::matchHeaders() — returns false when headers DO match and true when they DON'T. This breaks all consumers: fault_filter.cc (line 130) injects faults on the WRONG requests, RBAC filter denies the WRONG requests, and rate limiter throttles the WRONG requests. The empty-config case (line 41) still returns true, masking the bug when no header matching is configured.

Route cache clearing removed from ext_authz onComplete handler. When the external auth service responds with headers_to_set, headers_to_append, or headers_to_remove, the route cache is not invalidated. Downstream filters (including the router) use stale routing decisions based on the original headers, causing requests to be sent to the wrong upstream cluster after header modifications.

Required response header check inverted in encodeHeaders(). The condition 'if (status.ok())' sends a 502 BadGateway for ALL valid responses (those with a :status header) and allows responses MISSING the :status pseudo-header to pass through. This is a cross-file defect: the validation logic in header_utility.cc (checkRequiredResponseHeaders) is correct, but the filter_manager.cc response to the validation result is inverted.

Rate limit check inverted in postDelayInjection() — active_faults_ gauge is decremented when response rate limiting IS configured (causing double-decrement when onDestroy also decrements) and NOT decremented when it ISN'T (causing gauge leak). Over time, the leaked gauge reaches max_active_faults, blocking all future fault injections.

isRemovableHeader guard removed from ext_authz header removal path. Without this check, the external auth service can instruct removal of :-prefixed pseudo-headers (:method, :path, :authority, :scheme) and the Host header, making the HTTP request malformed. This bypasses the safety check when no decoder header mutation rules are configured, causing 400 errors or HTTP/2 protocol violations.
